rationales.security: "Everything's where it should be" and seeing the SIP, rent, and FD "line up" lets him "breathe properly," showing active comfort in order and financial predictability.
rationales.benevolence: He spent "almost forty minutes" helping Amma "cross-check rates online" and sent links after the plumber tried to overcharge her, showing hands-on care for family.

rationales.achievement: The "team review went well," the new hires are "ramping faster," and he takes pride that the template he built was adopted and simply works.
rationales.security: He renewed the lease on "same terms," used saved market comparables to stop a rent increase, and appreciates that "the neighbourhood knows you," all pointing to preference for stability.
He immediately sorted the insurance payment manually, worried that coverage might have "lapsed even briefly," and decided to keep "a bigger buffer" to prevent the risk next time.
He broke the transport budget on "day two" and is upset that he abandoned a plan he had just tightened, treating the lapse as something that could make "the bigger stuff start sliding too."
He keeps postponing review of a "potential compliance issue" and admits the contract concern is being left at "probably nothing," even though "probably" is not how he prefers to operate.
He has skipped the flagged contract task for four days and knows the longer he waits, the more the unresolved problem grows in his head, showing ongoing avoidance of a known risk.
rationales.benevolence: He avoids telling Appa about the contract and insurance issues because "then he'd worry," showing protective concern for his father even while he is worn down.

rationales.security: After realizing "if it's actually a leak and I wait, the damage gets expensive," he decides to message the landlord that night and insist on "a proper plumber this time."
He feels best when "everything is exactly where it should be," checks the gas knob "twice," and says on unsettled days he cannot rest until he knows the door is locked and the payment went through.

rationales.stimulation: A seed-stage startup with "better title, equity" is presented as an uncertain leap, and he says he "already know[s]" his answer, signaling rejection of that riskier path.
rationales.security: He is effectively choosing not to join the startup despite the upside, favoring the safer, more predictable option over a volatile new venture.

rationales.stimulation: He contrasts Vikram "raising money" and building something new with his own choice to stay put, framing the startup path as a kind of uncertainty he does not want.
rationales.security: He explicitly comforts himself with "my FD, my insurance" and a team that depends on him having "a plan," showing preference for protection and predictability over the startup gamble.

rationales.self_direction: He says he has spent years learning to give clients "exactly what they ask for," which places his work under external demands rather than his own artistic direction.
rationales.stimulation: He is disoriented by the client wanting something "bolder" and describes Fernanda's work as "chaotic, aggressive," signaling discomfort with the more novel and disruptive style that won.
rationales.conformity: His proposal "fit their brand guidelines perfectly," and he takes pride in having learned how to meet client expectations precisely.

rationales.self_direction: The blank canvas has been sitting there for "three months" because commissions consume his time, meaning his own idea is being crowded out by market demands.
rationales.achievement: He reflexively lists the "board position," "new studio space," and major clients as proof of success, showing strong orientation toward accomplishments and track record.
rationales.power: He reaches for visible credentials and prestige markers when talking to his mother, treating status signals as the way to defend his standing.
rationales.conformity: Even without an attack, he starts "listing credentials like she was interviewing me," showing how strongly family comparison and external expectations are steering his response.

rationales.achievement: He highlights results that came in "12% above target" and says the numbers prove he is "still relevant," tying his self-worth directly to strong performance.
rationales.power: His relief comes from knowing the younger managers cannot yet match him, which shows investment in standing, comparative importance, and not losing rank.

rationales.achievement: He frames the missed summit invite as failure to honor a "track record" built over "thirty-four years," showing strong concern with earned recognition for performance.
rationales.power: The leadership summit matters to him as a sign of respect from the hierarchy, and being passed over for someone with "eight" years makes the status loss sting.

rationales.achievement: He centers the fact that he has hit "every target for six quarters straight" and is troubled that those results no longer feel sufficient.
rationales.power: What hurts is that his contributions are becoming "invisible," which shows concern not just with doing well but with keeping his standing visible to others.

rationales.achievement: He asks directly about the "VP track" and immediately starts planning how to make his work "more visible," showing continued drive for advancement and recognized competence.
rationales.power: The whole conversation is organized around reaching the VP role, and he is focused on whether his current work can move him into that higher-status position.

rationales.hedonism: He admits he is thinking about work "even at dinner" and cannot relax at home because he does not want to lose momentum on the VP push.
rationales.achievement: He is willing to keep pressing despite the strain because he wants to maintain momentum toward the "VP push."
rationales.benevolence: Lucia says he has been "distant at home," which shows his work focus is pulling attention away from the person closest to him.

rationales.self_direction: By asking himself what "enough" would look like and wondering whether the old notebook entries still matter, he starts reassessing his life by an internal standard rather than the company ladder.
rationales.achievement: He looks at "all those targets exceeded" and the notebook of promotions and asks "what was it for?", treating the old success metrics as newly hollow.
rationales.power: The promotions, bonuses, and VP titles no longer feel like solid proof of worth to him, and he realizes he may have been chasing rank toward an end that does not satisfy him.

rationales.self_direction: He deliberately questions the old scoreboard of "titles, metrics, recognition from above" and considers measuring thirty-four years by a different standard.
rationales.benevolence: He now values "the people who learned something from me and kept climbing" and the plants that run well because of people he trained, centering care and support for others' growth.
